interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - provides user-drawn waveform when WaveformType is Custom

interfaces.name: SliderPackProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Slider pack data source - provides step values when WaveformType is Steps

interfaces.name: DisplayBufferSource
interfaces.signalFlowRole: skip - UI communication point for waveform display, not in signal path

parameterGroups.label: Oscillator
parameterGroups.rationale: Core waveform generation parameters that determine the LFO shape and speed

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.default: dynamic (3.0 Hz free, Eighth note synced)

parameters.name: PhaseOffset
parameters.description: The initial phase offset of the LFO

parameterGroups.label: Tempo & Sync
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters controlling tempo synchronisation and clock behaviour

parameters.name: SyncToMasterClock
parameters.description: Sync the LFO to the master clock

parameterGroups.label: Triggering
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters controlling how the LFO responds to note events

parameters.name: Legato
parameters.description: Disables retriggering of the LFO if multiple keys are pressed

parameters.name: IgnoreNoteOn
parameters.description: Free run mode - does not reset the LFO phase on note on

parameterGroups.label: Envelope
parameterGroups.rationale: Fade-in envelope that ramps the LFO intensity after each note trigger

parameters.name: FadeIn
parameters.description: Fade in time after each key press

parameterGroups.label: Output
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters shaping the final output signal

parameters.name: SmoothingTime
parameters.description: Smoothing time in milliseconds to reduce discontinuities in the modulation signal

parameterGroups.label: Step Sequencer
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters specific to the Steps waveform mode

parameters.name: NumSteps
parameters.description: Number of steps for the step sequencer

standaloneModChains.name: LFO Intensity Mod
standaloneModChains.note: Scales the LFO output intensity. No linked parameter - applied as a post-processing multiply on the final output.

gaps.question: What is the complete processing order from waveform generation through to modulation output? Specifically: where does fade-in apply, where does smoothing apply, and where does the intensity chain apply relative to the waveform calculation?
gaps.context: The pseudo-code must show the correct stage ordering

gaps.question: How are the different waveforms generated? Are they lookup tables, computed per-sample, or a mix? How does the Custom waveform use the TableProcessor?
gaps.context: Determines whether to show waveform generation as a single node or expand per type

gaps.question: How does the modulation mode (Gain/Pitch/Pan/Global) affect the output calculation? Is there bipolar vs unipolar behaviour?
gaps.context: The LFO output semantics change based on where it is placed in the module tree

gaps.question: When TempoSync is enabled, how does the Frequency parameter change meaning? How does SyncToMasterClock interact with TempoSync?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the tempo sync system to set up rhythmic modulation

gaps.question: How do Legato, IgnoreNoteOn, and the fade-in interact? What happens on noteOn and noteOff?
gaps.context: Triggering behaviour is critical for understanding LFO phase and envelope reset

gaps.question: What happens when LoopEnabled is off? Does the LFO stop at the end of one cycle? Does this differ by waveform type?
gaps.context: Non-looping LFO is used as a one-shot envelope; behaviour must be documented

gaps.question: How does the Steps waveform mode read from the SliderPackProcessor? Is there interpolation between steps?
gaps.context: Step sequencer mode is a distinct sub-feature that needs separate documentation

gaps.question: How often is the frequency modulation chain sampled? Is it per-sample or downsampled?
gaps.context: Affects the accuracy of FM-style modulation

gaps.question: Is the LFO calculated at audio rate or at a downsampled control rate? Are there any expensive operations?

notes: Complex TimeVariantModulator with 7 waveform modes, two modulation chains, tempo sync, and a step sequencer mode. The Frequency parameter has both a tempoSyncMux and a modMultiply composite - one of only three parameters in the entire module set with a tempoSyncIndex. The IntensityChain has no parameterIndex, making it a standalone modulation chain. This module is monophonic - all voices share the same LFO output.
